UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user within the LocalBid Exchange platform, storing their profile information, verification status, and aggregated performance metrics as a seller.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: Unique username chosen by the user.

email.description: User's email address, used for communication and login. (Assumes external authentication system handles password and detailed authentication flow.)

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

locationLatitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the user's primary location, used for local discovery.

locationLongitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the user's primary location, used for local discovery.

isIdVerified.description: Indicates if the user has completed the ID verification process to earn a 'Verified' badge.

totalSalesCount.description: The total number of successful sales made by this user as a seller.

reliabilityScore.description: An aggregate score reflecting the seller's reliability, derived from buyer reviews (e.g., 1-5).

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the user registered on the platform.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines a category for product listings, enabling organization and discovery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A brief description of what items fall under this category.

ProductListing.title: ProductListing
ProductListing.description: Represents an item listed for sale or auction on the platform by a seller.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductListing entity.

sellerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the seller who listed this product. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ProductListing)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N ProductListing)

title.description: The title of the product listing.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

price.description: The asking price for direct sale, if not an auction.

currency.description: The currency code for the price (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to images of the product.

listingLocationLatitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the product's location, used for proximity search.

listingLocationLongitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the product's location, used for proximity search.

postedDate.description: The date and time when the product was listed.

status.description: The current status of the listing (e.g., 'available', 'sold', 'auction_active', 'auction_ended', 'draft').

isAuction.description: Indicates if the listing is an auction item.

startingBid.description: The initial bid amount for auction items.

auctionEndDate.description: The date and time when the auction for this item will end.

currentHighestBidId.description: Reference to the current highest Bid for this auction. (Relationship: ProductListing 1:1 Bid)

viewCount.description: The number of times this listing has been viewed, used for demand analysis.

isBoosted.description: Indicates if the listing has been boosted for increased visibility.

boostEndDate.description: The date and time when the listing's boost will expire.

negotiable.description: Indicates if the listed price is open to negotiation.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records the details of a completed or pending purchase, including commission and payment status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

buyerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the buyer. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction)

sellerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the seller. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction)

listingId.description: Reference to the ProductListing involved in this transaction. (Relationship: ProductListing 1:N Transaction)

finalAmount.description: The final amount agreed upon for the transaction before commission.

commissionPercentage.description: The percentage commission applied to this transaction (e.g., 0.08 for 8%).

commissionAmount.description: The calculated commission amount for this transaction.

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the transaction was finalized.

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'escrow_held').

paymentGatewayTransactionId.description: Reference ID from the external payment gateway for this transaction.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Stores a buyer's rating and feedback for a seller following a transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

transactionId.description: Reference to the Transaction this review is associated with. (Relationship: Transaction 1:1 Review)

reviewerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the buyer who wrote the review. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Review)

sellerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the seller being reviewed. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Review)

rating.description: The numerical rating given by the buyer (e.g., 1-5 stars).

comment.description: Textual feedback provided by the buyer.

reviewDate.description: The date and time when the review was posted.

Bid.title: Bid
Bid.description: Represents a bid placed by a user on an auction listing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bid entity.

listingId.description: Reference to the ProductListing (auction) that this bid is for. (Relationship: ProductListing 1:N Bid)

bidderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the user who placed the bid. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Bid)

bidAmount.description: The amount of money offered in this bid.

bidDate.description: The date and time when the bid was placed.

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Manages direct messaging threads between two users, often related to a specific product listing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

listingId.description: Reference to the ProductListing this conversation is about. (Relationship: ProductListing 1:N Conversation)

user1Id.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the first participant in the conversation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Conversation)

user2Id.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the second participant in the conversation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Conversation)

createdDate.description: The date and time when the conversation was initiated.

lastMessageDate.description: The date and time of the most recent message in this conversation.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a single message sent within an in-app conversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the sender of this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message)

content.description: The textual content of the message.

sentDate.description: The date and time when the message was sent.

isRead.description: Indicates if the recipient has read the message.

isFlaggedForScam.description: Indicates if the message has been flagged by AI filters for suspicious content.

MeetingPointSuggestion.title: MeetingPointSuggestion
MeetingPointSuggestion.description: Stores details about a suggested public meeting point for buyers and sellers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MeetingPointSuggestion entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation where this meeting point was suggested. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N MeetingPointSuggestion)

suggestedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the user who made the suggestion. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MeetingPointSuggestion)

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the suggested meeting point.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the suggested meeting point.

name.description: The name of the public place (e.g., 'Central Park Library').

address.description: The full address of the suggested meeting point.

status.description: The current status of the suggestion (e.g., 'pending', 'accepted', 'rejected').

suggestionDate.description: The date and time when the meeting point was suggested.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Records a user's report against another user, listing, or message for inappropriate content or suspicious activity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Report entity.

reporterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the user who submitted the report. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Report)

reportedEntityId.description: The ID of the entity (UserProfile, ProductListing, or Message) being reported.

reportedEntityType.description: The type of entity being reported (e.g., 'UserProfile', 'ProductListing', 'Message').

reason.description: A categorized reason for the report (e.g., 'Scam', 'Inappropriate Content', 'Harassment').

details.description: Detailed description of the issue provided by the reporter.

reportDate.description: The date and time when the report was submitted.

status.description: The current status of the report (e.g., 'pending', 'reviewed', 'resolved', 'dismissed').

UserBlock.title: UserBlock
UserBlock.description: Records when one user blocks another to prevent further interaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserBlock entity.

blockerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the user who initiated the block. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserBlock)

blockedId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the user who was blocked. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserBlock)

blockDate.description: The date and time when the block was initiated.

ListingBoost.title: ListingBoost
ListingBoost.description: Details about a paid promotion applied to a product listing to increase its visibility.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ListingBoost entity.

listingId.description: Reference to the ProductListing that is being boosted. (Relationship: ProductListing 1:N ListingBoost)

boosterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the seller who purchased the boost. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ListingBoost)

startDate.description: The date and time when the boost period began.

endDate.description: The date and time when the boost period will end.

paymentTransactionRef.description: Reference to the payment transaction associated with this boost purchase.

boostType.description: The type or tier of boost purchased (e.g., 'Standard', 'Premium').

cost.description: The cost incurred for this listing boost.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. Accessible by the user whose ID matches `userId` in the path, or by administrators. Other users can read public profile fields. The document's 'id' field must match the 'userId' path parameter for integrity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile, corresponding to the authenticated user's UID.

definition.description: A global, read-only collection of product categories. Accessible by all users for browsing and filtering listings. Only administrators can create, update, or delete categories.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the product category.

definition.description: Stores product listings that are currently 'available' or 'auction_active'. Publicly readable. Includes denormalized 'sellerId' for direct authorization checks for the listing owner. When a draft is published, it moves to this collection.

params.name: listingId
params.description: The unique ID of the product listing.

definition.description: Stores product listings in 'draft' status, private to the seller. Accessible only by the seller whose ID matches `userId` in the path. The document's 'sellerId' field must match the `userId` path parameter. Supports authorization independence for draft management.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the seller, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: listingId
params.description: The unique ID of the draft product listing.

definition.description: Contains bids for a specific auction listing. Includes denormalized 'bidderId' for bid ownership and 'listingSellerId' (from the parent listing) for authorization independence, allowing the listing seller to view bids without a 'get()' call. Only the `bidderId` can create or modify their own bid. The `listingSellerId` can read all bids on their listing.

params.name: listingId
params.description: The unique ID of the auction product listing this bid is for.

params.name: bidId
params.description: The unique ID of the bid.

definition.description: Manages direct messaging threads. Includes denormalized 'user1Id' and 'user2Id' as participants (effectively a membership map) for direct authorization checks. Only `user1Id` or `user2Id` can read/write the conversation. Supports listing a user's conversations via a query on these fields.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the conversation.

definition.description: Individual messages within a conversation. Includes denormalized 'senderId' and a 'conversationParticipants' map (containing `user1Id` and `user2Id` from the parent conversation) for authorization independence. Only a participant of the parent conversation or the sender can read/write.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the message.

definition.description: Suggested meeting points related to a conversation. Includes denormalized 'suggestedByUserId' and a 'conversationParticipants' map for authorization independence, allowing access to any participant of the parent conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent conversation.

params.name: suggestionId
params.description: The unique ID of the meeting point suggestion.

definition.description: Records purchase transactions. Includes denormalized 'buyerId' and 'sellerId' for authorization independence, allowing both parties to access their own transactions. Supports efficient listing of a user's transactions via 'buyerId' or 'sellerId' queries.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores buyer reviews for sellers. Path-based ownership by `sellerId` allows sellers to easily view all reviews on their profile. Includes denormalized 'reviewerId' for authorization independence, allowing the reviewer to manage their own review. 'transactionId' is denormalized for context.

params.name: sellerId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (seller) being reviewed.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique ID of the review.

definition.description: Centralized collection for user-submitted reports of inappropriate content or activity. Includes denormalized 'reporterId' for the reporting user's ownership and 'reportedEntityId'/'reportedEntityType' for context. Reporters can read/update their own reports; administrators have full access.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique ID of the report.

definition.description: Records when a user blocks another. Path-based ownership by `blockerId` ensures only the blocking user can manage their blocked list, achieving authorization independence.

params.name: blockerId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who initiated the block.

params.name: blockedId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who was blocked.

definition.description: Details about paid promotions for listings. Path-based ownership by `boosterId` ensures only the purchasing seller can manage their boosts, achieving authorization independence. `listingId` is denormalized for contextual lookup.

params.name: boosterId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (seller) who purchased the boost.

params.name: boostId
params.description: The unique ID of the listing boost record.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for Database Access Control (DBAC) to signify administrator roles. The presence of a document for a `userId` in this collection grants administrative privileges. This design allows for simple `exists()` checks in security rules to determine admin status, promoting authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of a user with administrator privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query Accessibility (QAPs) by applying a combination of path-based ownership, structural segregation, and strategic denormalization. Each collection is designed to maintain a homogeneous security posture, simplifying security rules and ensuring atomic operations.

1.  **Authorization Independence (Strategy A):**
    *   `UserProfile`, `UserBlock`, `ListingBoost`: These entities are directly owned by a user, and their documents reside within a subcollection under `/userProfiles/{userId}/`. The `userId` in the path directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, making authorization checks immediate without requiring a `get()` call to a parent or other document.
    *   `ProductListing` (drafts): Draft listings are stored under `/userProfiles/{userId}/draftListings/{listingId}`. The `sellerId` field in the document will match the `userId` in the path, providing robust ownership verification.
    *   `Conversation`, `Message`, `MeetingPointSuggestion`: For collaborative entities like conversations, the `Conversation` document includes `user1Id` and `user2Id` directly. For subcollections (`Message`, `MeetingPointSuggestion`), a `conversationParticipants` map (`{user1Id: true, user2Id: true}`) is denormalized into each document. This allows rules to check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.conversationParticipants` or directly against `senderId` or `suggestedByUserId` without fetching the parent `Conversation` document.
    *   `Bid`: Each `Bid` document includes `bidderId` (the owner of the bid) and denormalized `listingSellerId` (the owner of the auction). This enables rules to grant access to either the bidder or the listing seller without a `get()` call.
    *   `Transaction`, `Review`, `Report`: These entities include `buyerId` and `sellerId` (for `Transaction`), `reviewerId` and `sellerId` (for `Review`), or `reporterId` (for `Report`) directly within the document. This makes authorization checks self-contained.

2.  **QAPs (Strategy B & C):**
    *   **Structural Segregation for Listings (Strategy B):** Product listings are segregated into `/publicListings/{listingId}` for `available` or `auction_active` items and `/userProfiles/{userId}/draftListings/{listingId}` for `draft` items. This ensures that public queries for listings don't need to filter out private drafts, and security rules for public listings are simpler (e.g., `allow read: if resource.data.status in ['available', 'auction_active'];`). Drafts are strictly private by virtue of their path.
    *   **Path-Based Ownership (Strategy C):** Entities like `UserProfile`, `UserBlock`, `ListingBoost`, and `DraftListings` use `/userProfiles/{userId}/<subcollection>/` patterns. This allows users to easily list their own data using a simple `collection('userProfiles', request.auth.uid, 'subcollection').get()` equivalent, satisfying QAPs for user-specific data.
    *   **Membership Maps for Collaboration (Strategy C):** For `Conversations` and their subcollections, the `user1Id`/`user2Id` (or `conversationParticipants` map) enables efficient queries for a user's conversations (`where('user1Id', '==', request.auth.uid)` or `where('user2Id', '==', request.auth.uid)`).
    *   **Direct Denormalization for Global Collections (Strategy A & C):** `Transactions` and `Reports` are global collections but contain `buyerId`/`sellerId` or `reporterId`. This allows efficient listing of a user's transactions or reports via simple `where` queries on these denormalized fields, which satisfy QAPs for these shared collections without filtering issues in rules.
    *   **Global, Read-Only Data:** `Categories` is a top-level collection that is publicly readable, perfectly supporting QAPs as all data is intended to be accessible.

This design minimizes complex rule logic by leveraging explicit data structures and denormalized authorization fields, making the system more secure, debuggable, and performant for common query patterns.
